item.title: Re: This made it.
item.content:encoded: Ha! That's actually what I first thought of when I saw the title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first thing I thought of when reading this was Spy vs. Spy. Sure, the "Bond" in the title may be intended to mean something else, but I instantly thought of them as secret agents. Fun article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not even consistent within the article. It says "Amnesiacs" at one point and "amnestics" at another. I don't think people should make unauthorized edits, but I do think each article should be internally consistent. If the author (or someone higher up than me on this pyramid we call the SCP wiki) could fix that, I don't think it'd be an issue/breaking rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've reverted your edit. While I personally believe you are right, it is up to each individual author to determine which term they want to use. That said, using the wrong term according to your head canon is - while a bit sad really - a valid reason for downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you wrote amnesiacs instead of amnestics, fixed it, hope that's ok

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my God, is this He-Who-Made-Light and He-Who-Made-Dark in beetle form?!

I love it!

P.S. you can abbreviate the genus name to just T. after you've mentioned it for the first time. Make it a little more streamlined.

item.title: Re: This made it.
item.content:encoded:
Empathetic bonds, but truth be told, the title is actually a reference to an MTG card.

please don't hurt me

item.title: This made it.
item.content:encoded: Some people may have problems with this 1, but it's the first article I up-voted. 1 question: Is the "bond" in "Bond Beetles" a reference to empathic bonds, James Bond, or a play on those double meanings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much was I was going to bring up. The opposite of happy can be sad, or ennui or, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love SCPs where it's the last line that cinches my upvote. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really have any technical issues with this one. More than anything, I'm just personally underwhelmed by the concept, which is probably the result of too many talking animal articles over the years. Not my cup of tea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops. I'll get that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last line really made this for me.

However:

I'd be nice if they didn't get all depressed about it, though.

should probably be: It'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This wouldn't be nearly as good if they weren't beetles, and that last line is killer, I love it so much. The bit about their father is great.

the opposite is true

I'd like to see this clarified. Does it mean sad people? Angry? There's a lot of emotions that could be considered opposite to happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love love love this skip. Glad to see it doing well on the main list. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Webb: Then he granted you these powers, to take your fight to a grander scale?

SCP-2819-2: No. He turned us into fucking milkweed beetles.

I love this line. You deserve this +1.

By the way, is this part of the 'He Who Made Dark' mythos?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me think too much of that one Star Trek episode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll fix that as soon as i get home—check back in four hours and let me know if it's better then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short and sweet1, the only problem I have with it being Thekillerax's mention of the beetles' ability to communicate not being stated in the description.

Footnotes
1. Not so sweet I would upvote but just sweet enough for me not to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nowhere in the description does it mention that the beetles are able to hold conversations. You might want to add that in. Also, "they will fight to the death" sounds a little non-clinical. Other than those minor quibbles, it was quite nice. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still think the point is driven home better if the mood-altering effect wears off on its own.

But it's pretty good anyway, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this up. Good job man, liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda pseudophilosophical over there, but it made me smile at the end so that's a bonus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot more than I did originally. Is it perfect? No. But it's entertaining, and I'm glad you took my advice to cut out the intelligence scaling. The whole thing works a lot better for me now, personally. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, here's Bond Beetles.

Thanks to Baygull and ZakMarcus and weizhong and Riemann and…mmm…anyone else who looked at this before it got posted.

I know there was someone else I was going to mention by name but I guess I forgot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like I stated when I read the draft, the whole SCP really builds up to that one line at the end. While it's a great line, it's simply not enough to justify the entire article.

The article has improved significantly, but not enough for me to novote or upvote.
